stories.title: YG And The Game Deliver Epic West Coast Verzuz Battle
stories.summary: YG and The Game held a historic West Coast Verzuz battle on July 23, streaming live on Apple Music and YouTube. The Compton rappers celebrated their city’s hip-hop legacy with a night of iconic performances. Earlier that morning, authorities briefly detained YG during a search warrant at his Burbank building unrelated to the Drakeo the Ruler case.

stories.title: Charli xcx: Music, Fashion, Film review – slippery search into selfhood that hits just as hard as Brat
stories.summary: Charli XCX's new album, Music, Fashion, Film, marks a significant departure from her 2024 era-defining record Brat, incorporating a rock sound and exploring themes of selfhood, art, and ego. The album's 11 short, guitar-based songs are a response to the intense scrutiny and cultural phenomenon that Brat spawned, with Charli XCX confronting her post-Brat demons and questioning her public persona. The album's release comes after a two-year hiatus, during which Charli XCX accepted acting roles and married her long-term partner.

stories.title: Brush fire near S Olympia St forces evacuations in south Kennewick
stories.summary: A rapidly growing brush fire near South Olympia Street has forced evacuations in south Kennewick, Washington, with the fire spreading to around 20 acres. Level 3 "Go Now" evacuation orders have been issued for the Inspiration Heights area, with multiple firefighting agencies on the scene. Firefighters are working to prevent the fire from threatening homes and other buildings, with a Red Flag Warning in effect due to windy conditions and low relative humidity.

stories.title: Phoenix Cyclops revealed as MARVEL Tōkon: Fighting Souls First Year 1 DLC character
stories.summary: Phoenix Cyclops, Marvel’s iconic X-Men leader infused with the Phoenix Force, will be the first Year 1 DLC character for Marvel Tōkon: Fighting Souls, arriving in fall 2026. The fiery update reimagines Scott Summers with devastating Optic Blasts and cosmic abilities, blending his classic combat with Phoenix-powered devastation.

stories.title: Charli xcx, Tyla, Morgan Wallen & More: New Music Friday Guide
stories.summary: Charli xcx releases her new album Music, Fashion, Film today, marking her first major post-Brat project. Tyla and Morgan Wallen also drop new music, with Tyla’s second studio album and Wallen’s latest single following his live preview. The lineup dominates playlists this weekend.

stories.title: Why Rogers and Palmer will thrive together in Alonso's Chelsea
stories.summary: Chelsea's new signing Morgan Rogers is set to reunite with his close friend Cole Palmer, who he has been eager to play with for a while. The two players are expected to thrive together under new manager Xabi Alonso, who will need to find a system that accommodates their similar profiles. With Rogers' versatile skillset and ability to adapt to different roles, Alonso will face a challenge in setting up his side this season.

stories.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
stories.summary: A rare black-crowned night heron has been spotted at the National Trust's Holnicote Estate in Somerset, a species rarely seen in the UK, with conservationists attributing the sighting to the introduction of new freshwater habitats. The estate's efforts to restore wetlands have also provided a refuge for other wildlife, including water voles, otters, pine martens, and white-tailed eagles. This comes as the UK has lost over 97% of its wetlands through drainage and changes in land management.

stories.title: Trump's new global tariff draws rebukes from trade partners over forced-labor justification
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has imposed new global tariffs on 60 economies, covering 99.4% of American imports, citing their failure to ban goods made with forced labor. The tariffs, ranging from 10% to 12.5%, target the US' top trade partners, including those in Australia, Brazil, and other countries that have not enforced such bans. The move replaces a temporary tariff and provides a more durable legal foundation for the administration's trade policy.

stories.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
stories.summary: A new play area at Winchester Science Centre in Hampshire aims to educate children about England's rare chalk streams, with over 80% of the world's chalk streams found in the country. The prolonged hot and dry weather is putting pressure on the wildlife that inhabits these waterways, prompting conservation efforts. The play zone is designed to encourage children to connect with the environment, potentially inspiring future conservationists.

stories.title: Vyner braced for eye-opening Wrexham popularity in US
stories.summary: Wrexham football club is set to face a massive following in the United States, with three upcoming games expected to attract huge crowds. The Welsh club, owned by Ryan Reynolds and Rob McElhenney, has previously toured America twice, drawing large crowds due to their global profile. Zak Vyner, a Wrexham defender, is anticipating an "eye-opening" experience, with fans in the US reportedly ten times more enthusiastic than those in Europe.

stories.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
stories.summary: A US study has found that replacing gas stoves with electric ones can lead to a significant reduction in asthma symptoms, with benefits comparable to those of certain asthma medications. The study, which initially aimed to investigate 1,200 households near Cleveland, Ohio, was scaled down to 72 homes after funding was terminated early. As a result, researchers detected a substantial 70% drop in hospital admissions and days off from work or school among participants.

stories.title: Urgent need for more security after physical attacks and abuse, say MPs’ staff
stories.summary: Staff employed by British MPs are demanding enhanced security measures following a surge in physical attacks and threatening communications, with nearly 80% of over 500 surveyed staff members reporting to have received threatening messages. The alarming trend has prompted Prime Minister Andy Burnham to announce a review of MPs' security, amid concerns for staff safety and well-being. The review comes in the wake of a murder investigation into the death of former Conservative minister Ann Widdecombe.

stories.title: My generation shouldn’t be scared of a care tax – we’re paying one already | Gaby Hinsliff
stories.summary: Generation X now faces the dual burden of caring for aging parents while raising their own children, with many juggling full-time jobs and long commutes. A growing care crisis is pushing families to spend financial and emotional resources they can’t afford, prompting calls for political action. Prime Minister Andy Burnham has signaled his intent to address social care funding amid rising public demand.

stories.title: Who am I? Guess Premier League star No 4
stories.summary: A new Premier League star is the subject of today’s "Who am I?" quiz by BBC Sport. Players must guess the footballer using as few clues as possible, with three correct answers considered a strong score. The challenge continues daily on BBC’s dedicated football quiz pages.

stories.title: Female veterans support group makes national impact
stories.summary: A UK-based support group for female veterans is making a national impact, with members sharing their experiences of post-traumatic stress disorder following deployments to war zones. The group's efforts are being supported by the Ministry of Defence, which has announced increased investment in NHS services for veterans. This move is expected to provide much-needed mental health support to the growing number of female veterans entering the military.

stories.title: 'Evidence lacking' for mental health monitoring tech
stories.summary: A UK inquiry into the deaths of over 2,000 mental health patients in Essex has found a lack of strong evidence supporting the use of Oxevision technology, which monitors patients using infrared sensors and cameras. The system, widely used in mental health wards, claims to have helped staff respond to 1,453 safety incidents over four years, but experts from the University of Oxford have questioned its effectiveness, citing methodological flaws in the studies used to justify its introduction. The inquiry's findings raise concerns about the widespread adoption of Oxevision technology in the NHS.

stories.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners, citing forced labour concerns
stories.summary: The US has imposed tariffs ranging from 10% to 12.5% on 60 of its trading partners, accounting for the majority of its imports, over concerns they failed to properly address forced labour. The targeted countries include key economic partners such as the UK, China, the European Union, Canada, Japan, and India. The tariffs came into effect on Friday, marking the latest escalation in the global trade war under US President Donald Trump's leadership.

stories.title: A Million-Panel Project
stories.summary: Utah’s new Green River Energy Center, a massive solar and battery storage plant, went online in June 2026 with nearly one million panels and 500 batteries. The 400-megawatt solar and 400-megawatt battery facility will power Salt Lake City and surrounding areas. It marks a shift from the region’s historic coal mining industry toward renewable energy.

stories.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
stories.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

stories.title: Modi Caught Off Guard as India’s Economic Failings Inflame Young People
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was busy trying to put out other fires when a student movement suddenly exploded. There are no easy solutions to the problems behind it.

stories.title: ‘Sia: Nostalgic for the Present’ Review: A Concert Performance Film from 2016, but It Channels Sia’s Mystery and Power
stories.summary: Sia’s 2016 concert film Nostalgic for the Present showcases her iconic anthems like "Unstoppable" and "Titanium" through a faceless performance that amplifies themes of strength and feminine triumph. The film’s abstract visuals present her as a vessel for a larger force, creating a powerful, otherworldly experience.

stories.title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
stories.summary: The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.

stories.title: Burnham urged to 'build bridges' with sacked Starmerites
stories.summary: Andy Burnham needs to "build bridges" with the Starmerite minister he sacked during his reshuffle, Baroness Harriet Harman has told the Electoral Dysfunction podcast.

stories.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
stories.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court stuck down his original levies.

stories.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
stories.summary: A Singapore court has ruled that 19 individuals are guilty of baby trafficking, with at least 34 babies being sold for adoption, 12 of whom were sent to Singapore. The convicted traffickers are now awaiting sentencing, while adoptive parents in Singapore are left uncertain about the fate of the children, with many awaiting a decision on whether the babies will be allowed to remain in their care. The Indonesian government is expected to work with Singaporean authorities to determine the next steps for the affected children.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: Google has been fined €890 million by the European Union for allegedly favouring its own apps over rivals, marking the first major action under the EU’s Digital Markets Act. The fine follows two breaches where Google’s practices were found to limit consumer choice and give its services an unfair advantage. Google disputes the decision, warning it could harm services used by millions in Europe.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.
